word_list.word: better to live one day as a tiger than a thousand years as a sheep
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ tə lˈɪv wˈʌn dˈeɪ æz ɐ tˈaɪɡɚ ðˌænə θˈaʊzənd jˈɪɹz æz ɐ ʃˈiːp

senses.definition: used to say that it is better to live a short but meaningful life, filled with courage and boldness, rather than living a long but unremarkable life that lacks purpose or ambition

word_list.word: cowards die many times before their [death]
word_list.pronunciation: kˈaʊɚdz dˈaɪ mˈɛni tˈaɪmz bɪfˌoːɹ ðɛɹ dˈɛθ

senses.definition: used to imply that confronting challenges with bravery is better than letting fear hold us back and suffer the consequences of inaction, as it may cause emotional suffering similar to death
used to emphasize the importance of taking action and being willing to take risks in order to achieve great things in life

word_list.word: a bully is always a coward
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ bˈʊli ɪz ˈɔːlweɪz ɐ kˈaʊɚd

senses.definition: used to imply that bullies use intimidation to make up for their own feelings of weakness or vulnerability

word_list.word: a man of courage never (wants|needs) weapons
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ mˈæn ʌv kˈɜːɹɪdʒ nˈɛvɚ wˈɑːnts nˈiːdz wˈɛpənz

senses.definition: used to imply that a courageous person will rely on their inner strength and moral character to face challenges, rather than resorting to violence or intimidation

word_list.word: boldly [go] where no man has gone before
word_list.pronunciation: bˈoʊldli ɡˌoʊ wˌɛɹ nˈoʊ mˈæn hɐz ɡɔn bɪfˈoːɹ

senses.definition: used to imply the importance of embracing the unknown, taking risks, and pushing beyond the boundaries of what is currently known or accepted in order to achieve great things

examples.x: The startup is developing cutting-edge technology, boldly going where no man has gone before in the quest for innovation.

word_list.word: conscience makes cowards of us all
word_list.pronunciation: kˈɑːnʃəns mˌeɪks kˈaʊɚdz ʌv ˌʌs ˈɔːl

senses.definition: used to say that people's sense of right and wrong can make them hesitant or afraid to take action, even if it is necessary or beneficial

examples.x: The politician was afraid to speak out against the corrupt practices of his party, knowing that conscience makes cowards of us all, and that challenging the status quo could put his career at risk.

word_list.word: eagles fly alone, but sheep flock together
word_list.pronunciation: ˈiːɡəlz flˈaɪ ɐlˈoʊn bˌʌt ʃˈiːp flˈɑːk təɡˈɛðɚ

senses.definition: used to suggest that individuals with exceptional or unique abilities may stand out from the crowd and be isolated, while those who lack such qualities tend to seek safety in groups

word_list.pronunciation: fˈeɪnt hˈɑːɹt nˈɛvɚ wˈʌn fˈɛɹ lˈeɪdi mˈeɪdən

senses.definition: used to suggest that a person who lacks courage or is too timid will never be able to win the affection of the person they desire, and that boldness and perseverance are necessary traits for success in romantic pursuits

examples.x: Even though he was nervous, John mustered up the courage to ask his crush out on a date, remembering the proverb that faint heart never won fair lady.

word_list.word: he that stays in the valley will (not|never) get over the hill
word_list.pronunciation: hiː ðæt stˈeɪz ɪnðə vˈæli wɪl nˌɑːt nˈɛvɚ ɡɛt ˌoʊvɚ ðə hˈɪl

senses.definition: used to suggest that progress and growth require taking some degree of risk and making an effort to move beyond one's current situation
